name: Cinnamon Breakfast Braid
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Cold butter or margarine (cut into 4 pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 1/4 cup of the water, 1 tablespoon of the sugar, and yeast in a bowl. Stir to dissolve yeast and let stand until bubbly, about 5 minutes.
2. Fit the processor with the steel blade.
3. Measure flour, butter, remaining sugar, and salt into the work bowl.
4. Process until mixed for about 15 seconds.
5. Turn on the processor and very slowly drizzle just enough of the remaining water through the feed tube into the flour mixture so that dough forms a ball that cleans the sides of the bowl. Process until the ball turns around the bowl about 25 times.
6. Turn off the processor and let the dough stand for 1 to 2 minutes.
7. Turn on the processor and gradually drizzle in enough of the remaining water to make the dough soft, smooth, and satiny, but not sticky. Process until the dough turns around the bowl about 15 times.
8. Turn dough onto a lightly greased surface. Cover with an inverted bowl and let stand for 20 minutes.
9. Prepare cinnamon sugar while the dough is standing by mixing it evenly lengthwise down the center of each strip.
10. Pull one edge of each strip over the cinnamon sugar to meet the other edge of the same strip, pinch the seam to seal.
11. Braid the strips together to form a loaf. Tuck the ends under and pinch to seal.
12. Place the braid on a greased cookie sheet. Brush with beaten egg white and sprinkle with almonds.
13. Let stand in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
14. Heat the oven to 375°F. Bake until evenly brown and the loaf sounds hollow when tapped, for 30-35 minutes.
15. Remove from the cookie sheet and cool on a wire rack.
